title: The Alderman's Ruin
hookText: A corrupt alderman. A patron who wants him gone. You have thirty days, a sharp tongue, and the power of a well-placed lie. Welcome to the art of political destruction.
description: The alderman Edric Fenn controls the town through a web of loyal allies, informants, and fear. Your patron's candidate loses another term unless Fenn is discredited before the autumn tax rolls are signed. You have thirty days to plant the right story and make sure it reaches the right ears.
introText: The stranger came with the harvest wagons — quiet as a loose coin in a full purse. Lord Edric Fenn has held the aldermanship for nine years, and the tax rolls are fat with his favor. The autumn assessment is twelve days off, and your patron grows impatient. A single rumor, well-placed, can bring down what nine years built.
startingText:
YOUR GOAL: Destroy Lord Edric Fenn's reputation. Get his score below 30.

HOW: Right-click buildings to Observe who is inside. Right-click NPCs to Eavesdrop on relationships. Press R to craft a Scandal rumor and whisper it to a well-connected townsperson. Watch it spread.

WATCH OUT: If you are caught eavesdropping, you lose instantly. You have 30 days.

Lord Edric Fenn holds the aldermanship with an iron hand. Your patron's candidate loses another term unless Fenn is discredited before the autumn tax rolls are signed.
victoryText:
The petition arrived at dawn — seven signatures, including Prior Aldous himself.

Lord Edric Fenn stepped down from the alderman's chair before midday. He did not look well.

You were already three days' ride away when word reached the capital.

failTexts.exposed:
The Guard Captain's eyes found you across the Market Square.

"I've been watching you," he said. "The Tavern, the Chapel steps, the widow's door. You've been very busy for a traveler with no business here."

He didn't arrest you. He didn't need to. By evening, the whole town knew your face.
failTexts.timeout:
The autumn tax rolls were signed. Lord Edric Fenn's seal was already dry.

Somewhere the story you planted still traveled — changed, embellished, half-forgotten. But the window had closed. Your patron's patience had not.

strategicBrief.primaryConstraint: 30-day window — the autumn tax vote seals the outcome
strategicBrief.strategicHint: Edric's shield is his loyal allies. Erode noble and merchant faction trust to isolate him.

objectiveCard.mission: Your patron wants Alderman Fenn gone before the autumn tax vote. Destroy his reputation — make the town turn on him.
objectiveCard.danger: If you are caught eavesdropping, the town will know your face.
objectiveCard.strategyHint: Edric is too proud to believe rumors about himself. Target the people around him — damage faction sentiment among merchants and nobles to erode his support.
objectiveCard.firstAction: Right-click a building (try the Market) to Observe who is inside. Then right-click two NPCs in conversation to Eavesdrop on their relationship. Use what you learn to craft your first Scandal rumor (press R).

milestoneToasts.message: The whispers are taking hold — Edric's name is tarnished.
His reputation is cracking. Keep pushing.
Almost there — one more push and Edric falls.

midGameEvents.name: The Ledger
midGameEvents.description: A merchant has found discrepancies in Edric Fenn's tax ledger — sums that do not add up, contracts that were never honoured. The evidence is in your hands. What do you do with it?

choices.label: Circulate the ledger through the market
choices.outcomeText: The ledger passes from hand to hand. By evening, half the market quarter is whispering about Edric's accounts. But the Guard Captain is asking questions about where the document came from.

choices.label: Use it as leverage — keep it quiet for now
choices.outcomeText: You pocket the ledger. Knowledge is currency, and this particular coin buys you room to manoeuvre. Edric's allies sense something has shifted, but they cannot name what.

midGameEvents.name: The Feast Invitation
midGameEvents.description: Lord Edric has announced a public feast at the Manor — roast boar, Rhenish wine, and speeches about his nine years of service. It is a transparent attempt to shore up his reputation. How do you respond?

choices.label: Infiltrate the feast and gather intelligence
choices.outcomeText: You slip in among the servants. Over three courses you learn who truly supports Edric and who merely tolerates him. You leave with fresh intelligence and extra opportunities to act — but the Guard Captain noticed an unfamiliar face.

choices.label: Spread word the feast is a bribe
choices.outcomeText: By the time the first course is served, the story has already changed. 'A feast for the town,' they say, 'or a feast to buy silence?' The nobles shift uncomfortably in their seats.

midGameEvents.name: The Midnight Meeting
midGameEvents.description: A servant at the Manor has tipped you off: Edric Fenn is meeting a creditor from the capital tonight, behind closed doors. The meeting concerns debts he has kept hidden from the town council. You have a narrow window to act.

choices.label: Eavesdrop and spread what you overhear
choices.outcomeText: You press your ear to the wall of the lesser hall. The numbers are damning — Edric owes more than the town's annual tithe. By morning, two of his closest allies are having second thoughts. But the guard patrols have tightened around the Manor.

choices.label: Bribe the servant for details instead
choices.outcomeText: The servant's account is secondhand but safe — no risk of being caught at the Manor. The details are enough to seed a credible rumor, and the servant's trust opens a new line of intelligence.

easy.designNote: More time, more gullible NPCs, and a higher heat ceiling give new players room to experiment with the rumor system.

normal.designNote: Base values as balanced. No overrides applied.

hard.designNote: Fewer days, skeptical NPCs, faster heat. Edric and Bram are harder to crack — requires precise rumor routing through low-loyalty intermediaries.

title: The Plague Scare
hookText: A rival healer threatens your employer's livelihood. One whispered word — 'plague' — and the whole town will do your work for you. But whisper to the wrong person, and the Church will silence you instead.
description: A rival herb-wife has arrived with the autumn rains and is undercutting the midwife who pays your wages. Spread an illness rumor through enough of the townsfolk and she will pack her cart and leave before the market closes. But the Chapel watches, and Sister Maren will not stay silent if the story grows too bold.
introText: She came with the autumn rains and the smell of something sharp — wormwood, maybe, or something worse. Alys the Herb-Wife has set up her trestle by the chapel steps, and already three of Vera's regulars have crossed the square to her instead. Illness travels fast in this town. Faster still when someone gives it a name.
startingText:
YOUR GOAL: Get 7 or more townspeople to believe illness rumors about Alys Herbwife.

HOW: Observe the Market and Apothecary to find NPCs connected to Alys. Press R to craft an Illness rumor, then whisper it to a credulous, well-connected NPC. Watch believers accumulate in the Scenario HUD.

WATCH OUT: If Sister Maren rejects ANY illness rumor about Alys, you have 2 days before you lose. Use the grace window to reach 7 believers or counter-seed doubt — but keep the rumor away from her circle. You have 20 days.

Alys the Herb-Wife set up her stall near the Chapel — too near to the Midwife's usual customers. Illness rumors, in this town, spread fast.
victoryText:
Alys the Herb-Wife packed her cart before dawn. She didn't say why.

Three days later, Sister Maren declared publicly that the illness talk had been unfounded. But by then, the Herb-Wife was gone, and the Midwife's customers had come back.

failTexts.contradicted:
Sister Maren stood in the Market Square and named every NPC who had spread the rumor. She did not name you.

She didn't need to.
failTexts.timeout:
The autumn market closed without incident. Alys the Herb-Wife was still there on the last day, packing her unsold stock slowly, in no hurry at all.

The Midwife's customers had not come back. The twenty days were gone.

strategicBrief.primaryConstraint: Maren Nun — one rejection triggers a 2-day grace window
strategicBrief.strategicHint: Seed illness rumors through the merchant quarter — Sybil, Nell, and Greta are credulous and well-connected.

objectiveCard.mission: The herbwife Alys knows too much. Convince seven townspeople she carries plague — before the nun Maren sees through the lie.
objectiveCard.winCondition: 7 or more townspeople must believe the illness rumor about Alys.
objectiveCard.danger: Sister Maren is skeptical and watchful. If Maren rejects ANY illness rumor about Alys, a 2-day grace window begins — fail to recover and you lose.
objectiveCard.strategyHint: Seed illness rumors through the merchant quarter — Sybil, Nell, and Greta are credulous and well-connected. Keep the rumor away from Maren's social circle.
objectiveCard.firstAction: Observe the Market or Apothecary to find NPCs connected to Alys. Then press R, pick Alys as the subject, choose Illness as the claim, and whisper it to someone credulous like Sybil Oats.

milestoneToasts.message: Fear spreads — the lie finds its first believers.
Halfway there. The plague scare is real to them.
Almost enough — one more voice and panic takes hold.

midGameEvents.name: The Stranger's Cough
midGameEvents.description: A traveler has arrived at the gate, visibly ill — coughing, pale, leaning on a stick. The town is already nervous. You could use this.

choices.label: Link the stranger's illness to Alys
choices.outcomeText: You mention, quietly, that the stranger visited Alys's stall yesterday. By noon, two more townspeople have decided they believe the illness rumor. Fear is a fast teacher.

choices.label: Spread quarantine rumors to distract Maren
choices.outcomeText: Sister Maren rushes to the gate to tend to the stranger. While she is occupied, the illness story about Alys spreads unchallenged through the market quarter.

midGameEvents.name: Alys Fights Back
midGameEvents.description: Alys the Herb-Wife has had enough. She stands in the Market Square and declares her innocence to anyone who will listen. Her voice is steady, but her hands are shaking. The crowd watches.

choices.label: Twist her words — she protests too much
choices.outcomeText: You lean toward the nearest merchant and murmur: 'If she were truly innocent, would she need to say so?' The seed lands. But Alys's speech has reached one believer, who begins to doubt.

choices.label: Stage a distraction — pull the crowd away
choices.outcomeText: A dropped crate, a shout from the alley, a child's cry. The crowd turns away before Alys can finish. Her words dissolve into the market noise, unheard.

midGameEvents.name: The Chapel Vigil
midGameEvents.description: Sister Maren has called a chapel vigil to pray for the health of the town. The clergy gather at vespers and the pews are full. This is dangerous — Maren is paying close attention to every whisper. But the vigil has also drawn the devout away from the market, where your work is easier.

choices.label: Use the empty market — push illness rumors while Maren is occupied
choices.outcomeText: With the chapel full and the market quiet, the illness story spreads through the remaining stalls unchecked. Two more merchants decide they believe. But the vigil ends at dawn, and Maren will be watching more closely than ever.

choices.label: Attend the vigil and redirect the prayers toward Alys
choices.outcomeText: You kneel with the rest and, during the open intercession, you speak of 'the stranger's herbs' and 'the sick children at the well.' The congregation murmurs. Maren's eyes narrow — but she cannot silence a prayer. The clergy leave the vigil unsettled.

easy.designNote: More days, more credulous NPCs, and only 5 believers needed. Maren's rejection triggers a 2-day grace window — teaches the avoidance mechanic with a wider margin.

normal.designNote: Base values as balanced. No overrides applied.

hard.designNote: Fewer days, 10 believers needed, and Maren is slightly more curious (higher credulity = she encounters more rumors, increasing rejection risk). Requires routing through 3+ independent clusters.

title: The Succession
hookText: Two targets. One to elevate, one to destroy. A rival agent is already working against you. This is a war fought with whispers — and you are fighting on two fronts.
description: Lady Isolde Fenn wants her son Calder elevated — but Tomas Reeve blocks any clean path to power, and Lord Edric is not ready to step aside. You must raise one reputation and ruin another simultaneously, while a rival agent works against you. Twenty-five days, two targets — and if Calder's reputation drops below 35, you fail instantly.
introText: Lady Isolde received you in the lesser hall, away from the servants. Her son Calder is twenty-two, quick, and capable — everything a future alderman should be. His only obstacle is Tomas Reeve, who has the favor of half the merchant quarter. The winter festival is twenty-five days away, and a rival is already working against you. It only takes one night to make or ruin a reputation — but it takes all twenty-five to manage two.
startingText:
YOUR GOAL: Raise Calder Fenn's reputation to 75+ AND drop Tomas Reeve's reputation to 35 or below.

HOW: Use Praise rumors to boost Calder through his allies. Use Scandal rumors to tear down Tomas through the merchant quarter. Press R to craft rumors. You fight on two fronts simultaneously.

WATCH OUT: If Calder's reputation drops below 35, you lose instantly. A rival agent is actively working against you — praising Tomas and scandalizing Calder. You have 25 days.

Lady Isolde hired you to elevate her son. Tomas Reeve blocks any clean path to power. One wrong twist and Calder himself becomes the story.
victoryText:
The winter festival nominations were read aloud in the Town Square.

Calder Fenn's name drew a real cheer — not a polite one. Tomas Reeve's name drew silence.

Lady Isolde watched from her window. She did not smile. But she did exhale.

failTexts.calder_implicated:
The rumor had started as praise. You knew that.

Somewhere between the Tavern and the Chapel steps, it had changed.

Calder Fenn was now the subject of a story that no one believed was an accident. Including Lady Isolde.
failTexts.timeout:
The winter festival nominations were read at dawn.

Calder Fenn's name drew polite applause. Tomas Reeve's drew the same.

Lady Isolde's face, when you passed her in the corridor, told you everything about whether there would be further work.

strategicBrief.primaryConstraint: A rival whisperer actively opposes you — Calder's allies can be turned
strategicBrief.strategicHint: Two-front war — boost Calder through noble allies while seeding scandal against Tomas through the merchant quarter.

objectiveCard.mission: The old lord is dying. Put Calder on the seat by building his name and tearing down Tomas — but a rival whisperer works against you.
objectiveCard.winCondition: Raise Calder's reputation to 75+ and drop Tomas's reputation to 35 or below.
objectiveCard.danger: If Calder's reputation drops below 35, instant fail. A rival agent is actively working against you.
objectiveCard.strategyHint: Two-front war. Spread positive rumors about Calder through his allies while seeding accusations against Tomas through the merchant quarter. Tomas has weak loyalties — rumors stick.
objectiveCard.firstAction: Observe a building to find Calder's allies. Press R to craft a Praise rumor about Calder and whisper it to a well-connected NPC. Then craft a Scandal about Tomas and seed it through the merchants.

milestoneToasts.message: The ground shifts — Calder rises as Tomas stumbles.
The scales are tipping. Stay the course.
Victory is close — finish what you started.

midGameEvents.name: The Debt Letter
midGameEvents.description: A crumpled letter has surfaced at the Tavern — addressed to a moneylender in the capital, signed by Tomas Reeve. The debts are substantial. This could be the weapon you need, but the rival will notice.

choices.label: Publish it widely — let the whole town see
choices.outcomeText: By sundown, copies of the letter are pinned to the Tavern door and the Market notice board. Tomas's reputation takes a sharp blow. But the rival, sensing your hand, redoubles their efforts.

choices.label: Show it privately to key merchants
choices.outcomeText: You share the letter with Aldric Vane and Marta Coin over quiet drinks. The damage is smaller but surgical — and Tomas's allies begin to wonder if their loyalty is misplaced.

midGameEvents.name: The Merchant Pact
midGameEvents.description: Aldric Vane has called a private meeting of the merchant guild at the Tavern. The question: should the guild publicly back Calder or Tomas? Aldric is undecided and open to persuasion. His choice will ripple through the entire merchant quarter.

choices.label: Convince Aldric that Calder is good for trade
choices.outcomeText: Over ale and ledger-talk, you make the case: a Fenn alderman means stable contracts, predictable tariffs, no sudden reforms. Aldric leans forward. By the end of the night, the guild's informal endorsement drifts Calder's way. Tomas's merchant friends are suddenly less sure of their man.

choices.label: Poison the well — make the guild distrust Tomas
choices.outcomeText: You arrive early and leave a rumor at each table: Tomas has been making promises to the capital's moneylenders that would undercut local traders. By the time the meeting starts, the mood has turned. Tomas's reputation takes a quiet hit, and the rival senses something has shifted.

midGameEvents.name: Calder's Blunder
midGameEvents.description: Calder Fenn has publicly insulted a merchant's weights in the Market Square. The merchant is furious, and the crowd is watching. Left alone, this will damage Calder's reputation. You must act quickly.

choices.label: Reframe it as boldness — Calder fights for honest trade
choices.outcomeText: You work the crowd: 'Did you see that? The boy has spine. That's what this town needs — someone who calls out cheats to their face.' The commons warm to him. The nobles raise an eyebrow.

choices.label: Have Lady Isolde smooth things over quietly
choices.outcomeText: Lady Isolde sends wine and an apology within the hour. The merchant is mollified. More importantly, the rival's planned attack is delayed — Isolde's intervention has shifted the conversation.

easy.designNote: Wider thresholds, later/weaker rival, more days. The two-front war is still present but the margins are forgiving.

normal.designNote: Base values as balanced. No overrides applied.

hard.designNote: Tighter win thresholds, earlier/stronger rival, Calder starts lower and Tomas starts higher. Tighter fail threshold since players can see it coming. Requires near-perfect rumor routing on both fronts simultaneously.

title: The Holy Inquisition
hookText: They came for three innocent people. You owe them everything. Now turn the town's own voice into a shield — because the Inquisitor will not stop, and neither can you.
description: An inquisitor has arrived with sealed orders and the weight of the Church behind him. Three townsfolk stand accused of heresy — and you owe each of them a debt that cannot be repaid in coin. Twenty days to keep their names clean, their reputations intact, and the inquisitor's stories from taking root.
introText:
Brother Cornelius arrived at vespers with three names on a writ and the Bishop's seal in red wax. By morning, the chapel steps were crowded with the fearful and the curious. Aldous Prior looked ill. Vera Midwife did not come to market. Finn Monk was seen packing a bag.

You know what happens when the Church names someone. The town does the rest.

You have twenty days. You have your wits. And you have a voice that can vouch for the innocent — if you spend it wisely.
startingText:
YOUR GOAL: Keep all three accused — Aldous Prior, Vera Midwife, and Finn Monk — above 45 reputation for 20 days.

HOW: This is purely defensive. Press R to craft Praise rumors about the accused, then whisper them to well-connected NPCs. Counter-spread faster than the Inquisitor can slander. Prioritize whoever is closest to the danger zone.

WATCH OUT: If ANY of the three drops below 40 reputation, you lose instantly. Between 40-44 is a danger zone — not yet fatal, but they must be back above 45 by the deadline. The Inquisitor seeds accusations on a cooldown that accelerates over time — he gets faster as the deadline approaches.

Brother Cornelius arrived with a sealed writ and three names. The charges are vague — heresy, improper doctrine, unsanctioned healing. Vague enough to stick. You owe these people.
victoryText:
Brother Cornelius left at dawn on the twenty-first day, his writ unsigned.

The Bishop's clerk recorded the outcome as 'insufficient evidence.' But you knew the truth — there had been plenty of evidence. It simply hadn't been believed.

Aldous Prior resumed his sermons. Vera Midwife reopened her door. Finn Monk stopped packing.

The town had chosen its own.

failTexts.reputation_collapsed:
The crowd gathered at the chapel steps before Brother Cornelius had even finished his morning prayers.

They came with stories — some true, most not, all believed. By evening, the writ was signed.

You watched from the edge of the square as the accused were led away. You had tried. The town had chosen differently.
failTexts.timeout:
Twenty days passed. Brother Cornelius presented his findings to the Bishop's clerk with the quiet satisfaction of a man whose work was already done.

The stories had taken root. The names on the writ were already ruined in every way that mattered. The formal verdict was a formality.

strategicBrief.goalLabel: Keep Finn, Vera, and Aldous above 48
strategicBrief.primaryConstraint: The Inquisitor accelerates — accusations come faster each day
strategicBrief.strategicHint: Prioritize Finn Monk — he absorbs slander most readily. Counter-spread positive character-witness rumors faster than the Inquisitor.

objectiveCard.mission: The Inquisitor is coming for three innocent people. Defend their reputations against a tide of heresy accusations — if any one falls below 48, you have failed them.
objectiveCard.winCondition: Keep Aldous Prior, Vera Midwife, and Finn Monk above 48 reputation at the deadline.
objectiveCard.danger: Finn is the most vulnerable — impressionable and wavering in loyalty, he absorbs slander more readily than the others. The Inquisitor is a relentless opponent who actively spreads accusations on a cooldown that accelerates over time.
objectiveCard.strategyHint: This is defensive. Counter-spread positive character-witness rumors faster than the Inquisitor. Prioritize protecting Finn Monk — then Vera.
objectiveCard.firstAction: Check the Scenario HUD to see who is closest to 48. Press R, choose the most threatened NPC as the subject, select Praise as the claim, and whisper it to their strongest ally.

milestoneToasts.message: A quarter done — your charges still stand. Hold the line.
Halfway through the fire. The Inquisitor hasn't broken through.

midGameEvents.name: A Noble Speaks
midGameEvents.description: A minor noble — one who owes Aldous a favour from years past — has stood up at the Market Square and spoken in defence of the accused. The crowd listened. You can build on this momentum, but you must choose how.

choices.label: Rally behind the noble — focus on the weakest accused
choices.outcomeText: You direct the noble's allies toward the most endangered name on the writ. The defence is concentrated and effective. One reputation steadies.

choices.label: Ask the noble to vouch for all three accused
choices.outcomeText: The noble agrees, but spreading the defence thins it. All three accused gain some shelter — but the Inquisitor, sensing organised resistance, quickens his pace.

midGameEvents.name: Forged Evidence
midGameEvents.description: Brother Cornelius has produced a document — a confession, he claims, signed by one of the accused. You recognise the forgery. But challenging it publicly will draw the Inquisitor's full attention to that name.

choices.label: Challenge the evidence publicly — expose the forgery
choices.outcomeText: You stand in the chapel and name the forgery for what it is. The document is discredited. But Brother Cornelius fixes his gaze on the accused — he will not forget this humiliation.

choices.label: Counter with character witnesses — drown out the evidence
choices.outcomeText: You send allies to speak for the targeted accused. Their reputation recovers. But the noise draws attention away from the other two, and whispers find fertile ground.

midGameEvents.name: The Breaking Point
midGameEvents.description: The pressure has told. The most vulnerable of the accused has been seen weeping in the chapel at midnight, whispering about surrender. Brother Cornelius knows — he is circling, waiting for a public confession. If the accused breaks, the other two will follow. You must intervene now.

choices.label: Rally the accused — remind them what they are fighting for
choices.outcomeText: You find them in the chapel before dawn. Your words are quiet but firm: the town needs them, the evidence is fabricated, and three more days is all you need. They straighten. The confession does not come. But the Inquisitor, sensing his prey slipping away, redoubles his efforts against all three.

choices.label: Redirect the Inquisitor — give him a false lead
choices.outcomeText: You plant a rumor about a fourth name — someone unconnected, someone disposable. Brother Cornelius takes the bait and spends two days investigating a phantom. The accused have breathing room. But the weakest among them is still shaken, and the reprieve is temporary.

easy.designNote: Higher starting reps, lower thresholds, slower/weaker inquisitor, more days. Defensive play is still required but the buffer is much wider.

normal.designNote: Base values as balanced. No overrides applied.

hard.designNote: Lower starting reps, higher thresholds, faster/stronger inquisitor, fewer days. One missed defensive cycle can cascade into an instant fail. Requires triage.

title: The Election
hookText: Three names on the ballot. One patron's gold in your pocket. The autumn vote is twenty-one days away — and the whole town is watching. Make sure they see what you want them to see.
description: The autumn alderman election pits three candidates against each other: Lord Edric Fenn (the entrenched incumbent), Aldric Vane (the merchant guild's ambitious challenger), and Tomas Reeve (the civic reformer with common-folk appeal). Your patron has bet everything on Aldric Vane — and you have twenty-one days to make the town agree. Boost Aldric, undermine both rivals, and time your moves around the Prior's endorsement.
introText:
The election notices went up at dawn — three names nailed to the chapel door, the market board, and the town hall gate. Lord Edric Fenn's name was first, as always. Aldric Vane's was second, in fresh ink. Tomas Reeve's was last, written in a hand that shook slightly.

Your patron met you behind the Guild Hall with a purse and a warning: 'Vane must win. Not by a hair — by a margin no one can contest. And if the Prior endorses someone else, we are finished.'
startingText:
YOUR GOAL: Get Aldric Vane elected alderman.

HOW: Aldric must have the highest reputation among the three candidates AND reach 65+. Both Edric Fenn and Tomas Reeve must be below 45. Use Praise rumors for Aldric and Scandal/Accusation rumors against the rivals. Press R to craft rumors.

WATCH OUT: On day 13, Prior Aldous will publicly endorse the candidate with the highest reputation — that endorsement grants a +8 reputation boost. If Aldric isn't leading by then, you may lose the election. If Aldric drops below 30, instant fail. You have 21 days.

Three candidates. One election. Twenty-one days to make the town choose your patron's man.
victoryText:
The votes were counted in the Town Hall with the doors open and the crowd pressing in.

Aldric Vane's name was called three times for every one of Edric's. Tomas Reeve's supporters had already left.

The new alderman accepted the chain of office with a speech about honest trade and fair governance. Your patron watched from the second row, expressionless. But when Vane finished, the old merchant raised his cup — just once, just slightly.

You were already packed.

failTexts.aldric_destroyed:
The story had started as praise. Somewhere between the Tavern and the Market, it curdled.

Aldric Vane withdrew his candidacy at noon. His supporters scattered like starlings. Your patron's investment — and your reputation — went with them.
failTexts.timeout:
The votes were counted. Edric Fenn's name was called first, as always.

Aldric Vane came second — close enough to taste it, far enough to know it was over. Tomas Reeve's supporters consoled themselves with ale.

Your patron did not send for you again.

strategicBrief.primaryConstraint: Prior endorses the leader on day 13 — timing is critical
strategicBrief.strategicHint: Boost Aldric through the merchant chain while undermining both rivals — ensure he leads before day 13.

endorsement.designNote: On day 13, Aldous Prior publicly endorses the candidate with the highest reputation. The endorsement grants +8 rep. This creates a critical timing gate — the player must ensure Aldric leads by day 13 or risk the endorsement going to a rival.

objectiveCard.mission: Get Aldric Vane elected alderman in a three-way race.
objectiveCard.winCondition: Aldric Vane reputation ≥ 65 AND highest of the three candidates. Edric Fenn AND Tomas Reeve both below 45.
objectiveCard.timeLimit: You have 21 days. The Prior's endorsement happens on day 13.
objectiveCard.danger: If Aldric drops below 30, instant fail. The Prior endorses whoever leads on day 13 — a +8 boost that could decide the race.
objectiveCard.strategyHint: Three-front war. Boost Aldric through the merchant chain (Sybil, Nell, Greta). Undermine Edric through his weaker noble ties (Annit, Old Hugh). Undermine Tomas through his common-folk connections (Sim, Constance). Time everything around the day-13 endorsement.
objectiveCard.firstAction: Observe the Market to find Aldric's merchant allies. Press R to craft a Praise rumor about Aldric and whisper it to Sybil Oats — she is the most connected merchant. Then start seeding Scandal rumors against whichever rival is currently leading.

milestoneToasts.message: The campaign is gaining traction — Aldric's name is on every tongue.
Aldric is making his move — keep the pressure on both rivals.
Victory is within reach — close out the election.

midGameEvents.name: The Market Debate
midGameEvents.description: The three candidates will speak in the Market Square at noon. The crowd is large and restless. You can influence how this goes — but so can the rivals' supporters.

choices.label: Plant cheering supporters for Aldric in the crowd
choices.outcomeText: Aldric's speech lands well — partly because of the words, partly because of the enthusiastic applause that drowns out the hecklers. The crowd leaves talking about Aldric's vision. But Edric's allies noticed the planted supporters.

choices.label: Heckle the rivals during their speeches
choices.outcomeText: Edric stumbles over a question about the treasury. Tomas loses his thread when the crowd turns hostile. Neither recovers well. But the tactic is transparent, and some in the crowd wonder who organised it.

midGameEvents.name: The Bribery Scandal
midGameEvents.description: A sealed letter has surfaced — purportedly showing Edric Fenn paying the Guard Captain for 'services rendered' beyond his official duties. It could be the killing blow. Or it could backfire if it looks like a forgery.

choices.label: Release the letter through the merchant quarter
choices.outcomeText: The letter passes through the market like fire through dry straw. Edric's support among the merchants collapses overnight. But the Guard Captain is furious — and he is looking for whoever leaked the document.

choices.label: Show it privately to Aldous Prior before the endorsement
choices.outcomeText: The Prior reads the letter in silence. He says nothing. But when the endorsement comes, his words carry a weight that suggests he knows more than he is saying. The letter stays between you.

midGameEvents.name: The Final Rally
midGameEvents.description: The election is days away. Tomas Reeve has called a gathering at the well — a last appeal to the common folk. Edric is countering with a feast at the Manor. Aldric has nothing planned. This is the moment to seize.

choices.label: Organise a merchant rally for Aldric at the Market Square
choices.outcomeText: Sybil and Nell spread the word. By evening, the Market Square is full of merchants calling Aldric's name. The display of support shakes Edric's remaining allies. But Tomas's common-folk gathering draws its own crowd — the race is not over.

choices.label: Sabotage Tomas's gathering with a planted heckler
choices.outcomeText: A well-timed question about Tomas's record on the bridge tolls derails his speech. The crowd disperses muttering. Tomas's support among the commons fractures. But Old Hugh saw who planted the heckler — and he is no friend of yours.

easy.designNote: More days, easier thresholds, bigger endorsement bonus, slower heat. The three-front challenge is preserved but the margins allow recovery from mistakes.

normal.designNote: Base values as balanced. No overrides applied.

hard.designNote: Edric starts stronger, Aldric weaker, Tomas higher. Fewer days, tighter thresholds, smaller endorsement bonus. Requires surgical precision across all three fronts and perfect timing around the endorsement.

title: The Merchant's Debt
hookText: The Guild Master has been cooking the books. You have the proof. But his allies run every stall in the market — and if they find you before the truth finds them, you'll be the one who disappears.
description: Marta Coin has discovered that Guild Master Aldric Vane has been embezzling from the merchant guild's common fund for years. The missing silver has been covered with false ledgers signed by Annit Scribe. Marta has hired you to expose Aldric — but quietly. If Aldric's merchant allies discover your hand before the evidence takes root, they will close ranks and silence Marta instead. Ruin Aldric's reputation. Protect Marta's. And keep your heat low — in this scenario, the guards are on Aldric's payroll.
introText:
Marta Coin's hands were steady when she showed you the ledger — a real one, not the copy Annit files at the Town Hall. The numbers told a story that Aldric Vane had spent years hiding: two hundred silver marks, skimmed from guild dues, laundered through the blacksmith's accounts.

'I need him gone,' she said. 'But I need to still be standing when the dust settles. If his people find out I'm behind this, they'll ruin me before he ever sees a courtroom.'

She's right. Aldric's merchant network is tight. The Guard Captain takes his coin. You'll need to work through the cracks — the clergy, the commons, the NPCs who owe Aldric nothing.
startingText:
YOUR GOAL: Expose Guild Master Aldric Vane's embezzlement and protect your patron Marta Coin.

HOW: Get Aldric Vane's reputation to 30 or below while keeping Marta Coin's reputation at 62 or above. Use Accusation and Scandal rumors against Aldric, and Praise rumors for Marta. Press R to craft rumors.

WATCH OUT: Heat ceiling is lower in this scenario — Bram Guard exposes you at 55 heat (not 80). Aldric's merchant allies will actively defend him. If Marta's reputation drops below 30, instant fail (she's been silenced). You have 20 days.

The merchant guild is Aldric's territory. Route your rumors through the clergy and commons — they owe him nothing.
victoryText:
The guild audit was called on a Tuesday — at Marta Coin's request, seconded by the Prior.

Aldric Vane did not attend. His seat in the Guild Hall was empty when the real ledger was read aloud. The silence that followed was louder than any accusation you had ever whispered.

Marta Coin was elected interim Guild Master before the week was out. She did not mention your name. She didn't need to.

failTexts.marta_silenced:
The story changed overnight. Suddenly it was Marta who was the thief — Marta who had falsified records, Marta who could not be trusted.

Aldric's allies moved fast. By the time you understood what had happened, Marta's stall was shuttered and her name was poison in the market quarter.

You had underestimated how quickly a guild can close ranks.
failTexts.exposed:
The Guard Captain found you at the well, just after dawn.

'Aldric Vane sends his regards,' he said. Not a threat — a statement of fact. By noon, every merchant in the quarter knew your face and your purpose.

Marta Coin denied ever meeting you. She had no choice.
failTexts.timeout:
Twenty days passed. The ledger sat in Marta's locked chest, still waiting for the right moment.

The right moment never came. Aldric Vane's reputation was bruised but intact. The guild closed its books for the season, and with them, the window for an audit.

Marta Coin paid you the second half of your fee without a word. You both knew it had been wasted.

strategicBrief.primaryConstraint: Heat ceiling 55 — guards are on Aldric's payroll
strategicBrief.strategicHint: Route accusations through clergy and commons — Aldric's merchant allies actively counter your market-quarter rumors.

guildDefenseConfig.designNote: Aldric's merchant allies actively spread praise rumors about him every 3 days starting day 5. This creates a defensive wall the player must outpace. Routing accusations through non-merchant channels (clergy, commons) bypasses the defense.

objectiveCard.mission: Expose Guild Master Aldric Vane's embezzlement while protecting your patron Marta Coin.
objectiveCard.winCondition: Get Aldric Vane's reputation to 30 or below. Keep Marta Coin's reputation at 62 or above.
objectiveCard.danger: Heat ceiling is 55 (not 80) — the guards are on Aldric's payroll. If Marta's reputation drops below 30, instant fail. Aldric's merchant allies actively defend him with praise rumors every 3 days.
objectiveCard.strategyHint: The merchant quarter is Aldric's stronghold — his allies will actively counter your rumors there. Route accusations through the clergy chain (Aldous → Maren → Vera → Constance) and the commons (Nell, Greta, Old Hugh). Boost Marta through her connections to Sim Carter and Nell Picker.
objectiveCard.firstAction: Observe the Chapel to find clergy NPCs disconnected from Aldric. Press R to craft an Accusation about Aldric (embezzlement) and whisper it to Finn Monk or Constance Widow — they have low loyalty and high connections outside the merchant quarter.

milestoneToasts.message: The first cracks appear — whispers of missing silver spread.
Halfway there. Aldric's allies are starting to doubt him.
The guild is turning — one final push and the truth wins.

midGameEvents.name: The Real Ledger
midGameEvents.description: Marta has given you the real ledger — the one that shows every missing silver mark, every falsified entry. Hard proof. But using it will put Marta's name on the evidence trail.

choices.label: Copy the key pages and leak them anonymously
choices.outcomeText: The copied pages appear on the chapel notice board overnight. No name attached. By morning, Aldous Prior is reading them with a furrowed brow. Aldric's merchant allies scramble to call them forgeries — but the numbers are too precise to dismiss.

choices.label: Hold the ledger — use it to turn Annit Scribe
choices.outcomeText: You show Annit the real ledger. Her face goes pale. She signed those false entries — she knows what that means. She won't speak against Aldric publicly. Not yet. But her loyalty wavers, and she stops defending him.

midGameEvents.name: Aldric's Counterstrike
midGameEvents.description: Aldric has heard the whispers. He has called a guild meeting at the Town Hall — and he intends to accuse Marta of jealousy, slander, and mismanaging her own accounts. If he succeeds, Marta's reputation will take a devastating blow.

choices.label: Intercept with character witnesses for Marta
choices.outcomeText: You've spent the last days building Marta's reputation. Now it pays off. When Aldric makes his accusations, Sim Carter stands and speaks for Marta. Then Nell Picker. Then Constance Widow. Aldric's attack falters against a wall of testimony.

choices.label: Let Aldric speak — then present counter-evidence publicly
choices.outcomeText: You wait. Aldric makes his case — convincing, polished, full of righteous anger. And then you step forward with the copied ledger pages. The silence that follows is devastating. But Aldric's allies now know exactly who to blame.

midGameEvents.name: The Guild Vote
midGameEvents.description: Marta has enough support to force a confidence vote on the Guild Master's accounts. But Aldric's defenders are mobilising — Sybil and Rufus are calling in every favour they are owed. The vote could go either way.

choices.label: Rally Marta's supporters to attend the vote in force
choices.outcomeText: Sim Carter, Nell Picker, and Constance Widow arrive early and take prominent seats. Their presence emboldens the undecided — when the hands go up, Marta's motion passes by three votes. Aldric's face is stone. The audit will happen.

choices.label: Leak Annit Scribe's involvement to fracture Aldric's defence
choices.outcomeText: The whisper reaches Rufus Bolt first: Annit signed the false ledgers. Rufus confronts Annit privately. She breaks. By the time the vote is called, two of Aldric's four defenders have gone quiet. But Aldric knows someone is pulling strings.

easy.designNote: Higher heat ceiling, easier thresholds, slower guild defense. The stealth challenge is present but forgiving. More time to build Marta's reputation before Aldric's counterstrike.

normal.designNote: Base values as balanced. No overrides applied.

hard.designNote: Aldric starts stronger and better defended. Marta starts weaker. Tighter heat ceiling (50), faster guild defense (every 2 days), and 18-day deadline. Requires near-perfect routing through non-merchant channels and constant Marta-boosting.
